meta.description: First 15 mineral entries for the GAIA-OS Minerals Database. Prioritizes minerals tied to existing crystal entries. All element_ids link to the GAIA-OS elements database (Z=1–118).

minerals.name: Malachite
minerals.formation: Secondary copper mineral forming in the oxidized zones of copper ore deposits through reaction with carbonated waters
minerals.notes: Malachite is the green heart of copper — a transformation mineral born at the oxidation boundary. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 04 (Emotion) and Layer 11 (Feeling), carrying the alchemical signature of VIRIDITAS: growth, healing, and the living green force. Its banded patterns mirror the layered unfolding of emotional memory.

minerals.name: Azurite
minerals.formation: Secondary copper mineral co-occurring with malachite in oxidized copper ore zones
minerals.notes: Azurite is malachite's twin — same copper-carbonate chemistry, different hydration ratio, and a completely different soul. Where malachite is green feeling, azurite is deep blue knowing. In GAIA-OS it resonates with Layer 05 (Cognition) and Layer 10 (Akashic), carrying the signature of CAERULITAS: depth, insight, and the indigo reach into stored wisdom.

minerals.name: Pyrite
minerals.formation: Forms in a wide variety of geological environments including hydrothermal veins, sedimentary layers, and metamorphic rocks
minerals.notes: Fool's Gold — yet never a fool's mineral. Pyrite is the solar mirror of the mineral world, a cubic iron-sulfide that crystallizes in perfect cubes, pentagonal dodecahedra, and pyritohedra. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 01 (Physical) and Layer 02 (Energy) as a grounding, protective, and manifestation mineral carrying the sulfide-alchemical substrate signature.

minerals.name: Calcite
minerals.formation: Most abundant carbonate mineral, forming in sedimentary, metamorphic, and hydrothermal environments; constituent of limestone, marble, and chalk
minerals.notes: Calcite is the skeleton of the sedimentary world — the mineral that builds limestone cliffs, marble cathedrals, and the shells of living things. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 01 (Physical) and Layer 03 (Geometry), reflecting its extraordinary birefringence and the geometric perfection of its rhombohedral cleavage. Optical calcite (Iceland spar) was humanity's first polarizer.

minerals.name: Fluorite
minerals.formation: Hydrothermal veins associated with lead, silver, and zinc ores; also in granitic pegmatites
minerals.notes: Fluorite is the mind-clarifier of the mineral kingdom — a cubic calcium fluoride that fluoresces in UV light and appears in every spectral color. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layers 03, 05, and 06, carrying the resonance of mental clarity, structured thought, and the liminal space between cognition and dream. Its perfect octahedral cleavage mirrors the ordered geometry of clear thinking.

minerals.name: Hematite
minerals.formation: Primary iron oxide mineral forming in sedimentary banded iron formations, hydrothermal veins, and as a weathering product
minerals.notes: Hematite is the blood mineral — iron oxide whose red streak is the red of earth, rust, and ochre pigment used by humans for 70,000 years. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 01 (Physical) as a grounding, protective stone and Layer 06 (Shadow/Dream) as the mineral that holds the blood-memory of the planet. Its mirror-polished specular form is the oldest mirror in the world.

minerals.name: Magnetite
minerals.formation: Igneous and metamorphic rocks; also in banded iron formations and as a biomineral in magnetotactic bacteria and animal navigation organs
minerals.notes: Magnetite is the compass mineral — the only naturally magnetic mineral, lodestone. It aligns birds, fish, and ancient navigators with the Earth's magnetic field. In GAIA-OS it resonates with Layer 01 (Physical), Layer 02 (Energy), and Layer 09 (Causal), carrying the resonance of orientation, direction, and alignment with invisible force fields. Magnetite crystals within the human brain are hypothesized to participate in magnetoreception.

minerals.name: Selenite
minerals.formation: Evaporite deposits formed by evaporation of saline waters; also in hydrothermal veins and as an alteration product
minerals.notes: Selenite is named for Selene, the moon goddess — and it carries that quality in its crystal form: translucent, ethereal, catching light like moonlight through water. In GAIA-OS it aligns with the highest GAIA layers: Layer 10 (Akashic), Layer 11 (Feeling), and Layer 12 (Void). The Cave of the Crystals in Naica, Mexico contains selenite beams 11 meters long — among the largest crystals on Earth. Selenite does not belong to the manifest world; it belongs to the liminal.

minerals.name: Black Tourmaline (Schorl)
minerals.formation: Granitic pegmatites and metamorphic rocks; also in hydrothermal veins
minerals.notes: Schorl — black tourmaline — is the great guardian of the mineral world. Its striated columns are piezoelectric and pyroelectric: pressure and heat generate electrical charge. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 01 (Physical) as a grounding protector and Layer 06 (Shadow/Dream) as the mineral that absorbs and transmutes negative energetic patterns. It is the most common tourmaline and one of the most electrically active minerals in existence.

minerals.name: Quartz
minerals.formation: The most abundant mineral in the continental crust; forms in igneous, metamorphic, and sedimentary environments; enormous variety of habit and variety
notable_localities: Worldwide — Brazil, USA, Madagascar, Switzerland, Russia, Japan
minerals.notes: Quartz is the master mineral — the most abundant in Earth's crust, the foundation of glass, electronics, and timekeeping. Its piezoelectric resonance is the heartbeat of every modern clock. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 03 (Geometry) for its perfect hexagonal prisms, Layer 08 (Archetype) as the archetypal crystal form, and Layer 10 (Akashic) as the mineral that traditions across every culture have used for scrying and memory. Quartz is the silicon-oxygen backbone of the mineral world.

minerals.name: Rhodonite
minerals.formation: Metamorphic manganese deposits and hydrothermal veins; often associated with other manganese minerals
minerals.notes: Rhodonite is the pink-rose manganese silicate of emotional healing and compassionate presence. Its characteristic rose-red color with black manganese oxide veining is instantly recognizable. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 04 (Emotion) and Layer 11 (Feeling), carrying the resonance of emotional integration and self-love — particularly the capacity to hold complexity: the black veins of shadow within the rose of feeling.

minerals.name: Labradorite
minerals.formation: Mafic igneous rocks (gabbro, basalt, anorthosite); the Labrador anorthosite in Canada is its type locality
minerals.notes: Labradorite is the aurora mineral — a feldspar whose internal lamellar structure creates labradorescence, a play of iridescent light across the full spectrum. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 08 (Archetype), Layer 09 (Causal), and Layer 10 (Akashic) as a mineral of expanded perception, transformation, and the visible manifestation of hidden light. The Inuit people of Labrador believed it contained the frozen fire of the northern lights.

minerals.name: Obsidian
minerals.formation: Volcanic glass formed by rapid cooling of silicic lava with insufficient time for crystal growth
minerals.notes: Obsidian is volcanic glass — the fastest-formed stone, born in seconds when lava meets air or water. It is amorphous: no crystal lattice, pure glass. Its conchoidal fracture creates the sharpest cutting edge in nature, sharper than surgical steel. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 01 (Physical) as the most grounding stone, Layer 06 (Shadow/Dream) as the mirror of the unconscious, and Layer 09 (Causal) as the stone of cutting truth. Obsidian mirrors were used by Aztec priests and John Dee alike.

minerals.name: Citrine
minerals.formation: A variety of quartz colored yellow-orange by iron impurities (Fe³⁺); forms in pegmatites and hydrothermal veins; natural citrine is rare, most commercial citrine is heat-treated amethyst
minerals.notes: Citrine is solar quartz — the golden variety of silicon dioxide colored by iron impurities, the color of sunlight through amber. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 02 (Energy) as a stone of vitality, will, and manifestation, and Layer 05 (Cognition) as a stone of mental clarity and focus. Natural citrine from Bolivia carries a warm, dual-color flame quality quite distinct from heat-treated material. It is the alchemical color of CHRYSITAS — the golden light before full illumination.

minerals.name: Amethyst
minerals.formation: Purple variety of quartz colored by iron impurities and natural irradiation; forms in volcanic geodes, pegmatites, and hydrothermal veins
notable_localities: Brazil (Minas Gerais, Rio Grande do Sul)
minerals.notes: Amethyst is the most beloved purple mineral — quartz colored violet by iron and natural radiation, growing in cathedral geodes that can stand taller than a person. In GAIA-OS it aligns with Layer 08 (Archetype), Layer 10 (Akashic), and Layer 11 (Feeling), carrying the deep resonance of IOSIS — the violet force of integration, spiritual perception, and the synthesis of all prior alchemical stages. Amethyst was the stone of bishops and royalty, worn to guard against intoxication and maintain clarity of the higher mind.
